Video.summary: We kick off Season 2 in Tennessee where we stop in Nashville, Townsend, and Johnson City!!
Video.title: "Traveling Over 2,000 Miles to Fish HERE!"

Video.summary: This episode we make our way through the mountains of western North Carolina, where we stop in Asheville and Cashiers!!
Video.title: "Dry Fly Fishing HEAVEN (Wild Trout)"

Video.summary: In this episode Andy goes back to his home farm in Alabama for deer and quail hunting. Not only does he make incredible dishes with his former ‘masterchef’ contestant Alex which he serves to local children in need but he explains how hunting is never about “just the hunt” but about the camaraderie, friendship and memories made through the pursuit of the harvest.
Video.title: Wildbound - "Alabama"

Video.summary: After multiple days in Alaska, it was time for the last trip out to sea. The crew got to experience the sights on this trip, like God Valley and others. And of course catch more Alaskan fish.
Video.title: "The Final Voyage"

Video.summary: When tarpon don’t cooperate, it’s time for Capt. Justin and friend Ethan Register to have a competition for dinner options in the gulf’s grocery store.
Video.title: "Once Upon a Wahoo"

Video.summary: Rusty Creasey joins the Spring Thunder crew for an incredible turkey hunt in Oklahoma. The guys are blessed to fill 6 tags in a short time and have several memorable hunts.
Video.title: Spring Thunder - "Oklahoma"

Video.summary: Justin and Brandi with Big Dreams Outdoors finish up their slam hunting Merriam turkeys with 7J Outfitters in Wyoming. It was a tough week for Justin but persistence pays off!
Video.title: Spring Thunder - "Big Merriam Dreams"

Video.summary: Follow TTHA President/CEO Christina Pittman and her husband Matt this week as they chase Whitetails at Morrow Ranch. This is a hunt that they had purchased earlier at the Disabled Outdoorsman in the year to be able to spend some time in the woods. A few days away to relax and spend quality time with loved ones should always be a priority. Morrow Ranch is definitely a go to place for low fence Texas hunting.
Video.title: "Morrow Ranch"

Video.summary: David Blanton heads North to hunt turkeys with new friend Evan Putnam. David gets the true Maine experience catching lobster one evening and following up with an amazing turkey hunt.
Video.title: Spring Thunder - "David Maine"

Video.summary: Dan Cabela sets out to learn about the model of private ownership of wildlife in South Africa. Along the way, he participates in game capture, attends an auction and hunts to support a community.
Video.title: "Private Ownership of Wildlife"

Video.summary: Dan Cabela dives into the heart of one of the world’s largest game reserves - the Selous. He explores the synergy between hunting and conservation that is preserving this wild paradise.
Video.title: "A Conservation Legacy"

Video.summary: Illinois isn’t the first state you think about when it comes to waterfowl, but the Wentz Brothers have dialed in some honey holes over the years. On this episode, a slew of waterfowl experts put to the test some of the newest Winchester waterfowl ammunition during the great migration of the Mississippi Flyway in early December. We’ll also head north to the shores of Kodiak Island, Alaska with Chris and Casey Keefer as they push the limits of Winchester’s new Last Call Double Shot on fast-ripping sea ducks. - GUESTS: Zach Wentz (Team Winchester) Nathan Robinson (Winchester Ammunition) Ryan Swaner (Winchester Repeating Arms) Chris Jennings (Ducks Unlimited) Mike Brasher (Ducks Unlimited) Scott Olmsted (American Hunter) Travis Hall (Field & Stream) Chris Keefer (Team Winchester) Casey Keefer (Team Winchester)
Video.title: "Flight Proven"

Video.summary: Capt. Blair has some down time in Alaska, so what does he do? Heads to the beach to check out the salmon hatchery in Ketchican. They've met their quota for the year so the season is open, but the trick is how to catch the salmon. There hasn't been an episode like this yet.
Video.title: "Salmon Snagging"

Video.summary: When the captain calls and says the weather in Alaska is great, you don't waste the opportunity. So Capt. Blair and the whole crew head out for a day of fishing. With one camera on the boat, things got a little chaotic with all the catching going on.
Video.title: "Weather Window Day"

Video.summary: Capt Justin & Meredith’s beautiful Panama City Beach morning is met with a slack tide so a new path to redfish success is found under a popular bridge.
Video.title: "A Bridge Too Red"

Video.summary: If you had a blank check to build the best fishing boat possible, what would you create? Capt. Justin gets his wish, but it doesn’t come quickly or easily.
Video.title: "Dream Boat Build"

Video.summary: Capt. Justin takes a fellow guide on his new boat to see how it measures up. Using only public waypoints on his Humminbird unit, they find exploring to be as much fun as it is fruitful.
Video.title: "Fishing the Dream Boat"

Video.summary: Capt. Justin heads offshore, dodges storms and shows how the "Return 'Em Right" program is saving fish that experience barotrauma.
Video.title: "Happy Returns"

Video.summary: This week, we reflect on a tragic and sad memory in our lives as we remember a dear childhood friend of my son Nick, Nathan Wimsatt. Our families have been close for man years and shared a lot of time together around the soccer field, vacations, fishing trips, and much more. Nathan passed in a tragic car accident at only 18 years on April 29th, 2023. This week his Dad Brian is joining me on Lake Taylorsville where we'll share some memories and celebrate Nathan's life...that ended way to soon!
Video.title: "Life Is A Highway"

Video.summary: This week we're joined by our good friend and Worship Leader, Rob Key. A tremendous musical talent that's chased the country music dream, but found his calling as worship leader at Taylorsville Community Church. Rob's love for serving the Lord is his top priority, but he certainly enjoys the great outdoors and especially doing a little fishing.
Video.title: "Worship, Music, and Bassin'"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor are joined by Florida Keys guide Jeff Malone to find a productive wreck with which they enjoy the exciting variety of fish that wrecks can provide.
Video.title: "Jeff Malones New Wreck"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor are joined by marine scientist Nick Castillo to search for bonefish in Key Largo for both fun and study.
Video.title: "Bonefish Science With Nick Castillo"

Video.summary: With Montana in the rearview mirror the Flylords team crosses over into Idaho. In this leg of the trip they hit Blue-Ribbon trout waters with the folks from Henrys Fork Anglers and Silver Creek Outfitters. They also have a chance to sit down with the owners of these operations and hear what these shops and fly fishing mean to them. Before they hit the road to Utah the crew joins in on another conservation event at Payette Brewing Company.
Video.title: "SIGHT Casting RISING Trout in IDAHO"

Video.summary: It’s the final leg of the Fly Shop Tour and what better way to end it than with a trip down to see the crew at Fly Fish Food. We learn the unique story of how owners Clark “Cheech” Pierce & Curtis Fry started their shop and spend some time chasing hungry Cutthroat. Wrapping it all up the team puts on the final conservation event of the season with Fly Fish Food and Fish For Garbage, as well as spending some time behind the oars on the Green River with crew at Western Rivers Fly Shop.
Video.title: "The END of our 2500 Mile Road Trip"

Image.alt: "The END of our 2500 Mile Road Trip"
"The END of our 2500 Mile Road Trip"
"The END of our 2500 Mile Road Trip"

Video.summary: Join Gavin as he heads up to Maine the last week of May to chase finicky gobblers with a couple good friends.
Video.title: The - "3 TURKEYS In 3 DAYS | TURKEY hunting in MAINE"

Image.alt: The - "3 TURKEYS In 3 DAYS | TURKEY hunting in MAINE"
The - "3 TURKEYS In 3 DAYS | TURKEY hunting in MAINE"
The - "3 TURKEYS In 3 DAYS | TURKEY hunting in MAINE"

Video.summary: Dan Schmidt sits down with Auburn University professor Dr. Steve Ditchkoff to tackle one of the most debated topics in deer management: cull bucks. Is there real science behind the idea of removing “inferior” bucks to improve herd genetics, or is it more myth than management?
Video.title: "Cull Me, Maybe."

Video.summary: Mark Peterson and Earl Peterson are headed down to Kentucky for the opening of deer season. With this being the earliest that a bowhunter can hunt whitetails, it can result in a giant velvet whitetail harvest. After a fun night enjoying the Swon Brothers' music, it's time to dial the bow in, freshen up the Buck Bourbon at feed sites and hope that Brows or Kevins' buck show themselves. How will the story end on the Giant Deer named Brows?
Video.title: "2022 Kentucky Whitetail Velvet Opener"

Video.summary: It’s that time again—when the boys from StruttinBuck load up and head out of Arkansas, bound for Fairfield, Texas and the FE Hill Ranch. Another year, another hunt, and another chapter in a tradition that’s grown into something far bigger than the hunt itself. This annual father-son trip is built on early mornings, shared stories, and moments that stick with you long after the season ends. It’s the memories made over the years that keep calling them back—and this year will be no different.
Video.title: "FE Hill"

Video.summary: On this episode of Hunting Land Man Season 3, Slade and the crew head north to scout out, plan, and put in the plots to lay the groundwork for this falls hunting season in the Midwest!
Video.title: "Heading North, Missouri Food Plot Prep"

Video.summary: The 2024 turkey season has arrived. Slade and Bentley are already off to a great start. Bentley managed to take one down opening weekend for youth hunt. And Slade took one down opening day. This episode features an inside look into Slade’s life filled with real eastate and the great outdoors.
Video.title: "Thunder"

Video.summary: In this episode, Slade and the new producer (Tim Guidroz) go out on their first turkey hunt together, and even though the weather is not ideal, they get the job done on a Wilkinson County gobbler. We also get a look at what the United Country - Southern States Realty office looks like during turkey season, with interviews from Scott Lindsey and Caleb Armstrong, and three big properties closing in Baton Rouge.
Video.title: "Turkeys Vs. Real Estate"

Video.summary: In this episode, Slade takes us through his new investment property, and breaks down - what’s involved in taking a raw land tract and creating extremely desirable turn-key - hunting properties. - Caleb and Slade double up on some Wilkinson County gobblers for Calebs first turkey - kill ever.
Video.title: "Flips And Flops"

Video.summary: In this episode we take you behind the scenes of production for one of the biggest and baddest listings Slade has ever gotten - Southside Land Company in Jefferson County, MS. Hunter Phelps comes down from Kentucky to help film, and Slade puts him on 2 Mississippi gobblers.
Video.title: "Southside"

Video.summary: LBP Spotlights West End Boatworks and goes offshore fishing.
Video.title: "West End Boatworks"

Video.summary: LBP Spotlights Clay Shidler and the history of his charter business, Hang'Em High Sportfishing.
Video.title: "Clay Shidler"

Video.summary: Meet Ryan, a seasoned St. Petersburg guide, fighting tides, time, and the last great tarpon run of the season.
Video.title: "Ryan Harrington"

Video.summary: Meet Captain Lionel James — a respected Destin, Florida guide known for his upbeat personality, strong community ties, and love for fitness when he’s not on the water.
Video.title: "Lionel James"

Video.summary: We love our members. We spent some time in camp with Ron Chandler. Ron Recently renewed his TTHA Membership in 2022 and was awarded a member hunt for 2023. We spend time reflecting on what hunting means to Ron and spending time in camp at G2 with hunters alike.
Video.title: "Member Hunt"

Video.summary: Over the years we have been able to hunt with a lot of amazing people. This episode takes us to Las Palmas ranch to hunt with Payton Angelle. Payton was awarded this hunt from the amazing people over at Sky High for kids.
Video.title: "Las Palmas"

Video.summary: We love our college chapters. Hudson has been a long time member of TTHA as well as serving with the Texas A&M Chapter. We have a great time at G2 in pursuit of Axis deer.
Video.title: "A&M Axis"

Video.summary: Down in Sebastion, the monster snook wait. These fish are so elusive, men have gotten divorced chasing these things. These can be once in a lifetime fish, and even then it's not a guarantee.
Video.title: "Hunting a Mogan"

Video.summary: Kyle and Jeff join a local biologist searching for a white rhino whose tracking collar has failed, leaving it exposed to poachers. As they follow its trail through the African bush, the search builds toward a heart-pounding encounter that none of them will ever forget.
Video.title: "Africa Part 3"

Video.summary: Kyle takes to the water in a way few anglers dare, fishing from a Sea-Doo in search of the ancient lake sturgeon, a species that’s been around since the age of the dinosaurs and can live over 100 years. Hooking into one of North America’s largest freshwater fish from a personal watercraft proves to be a challenge unlike any other.
Video.title: "Extreme Lake Sturgeon"

Video.summary: Kyle and Jeff embark on a 20-day mission into the Everglades to find the invasive pythons that are plaguing the ecosystem. These apex predators have eliminated more than 90% of fur-bearing animals in their new home. Can they turn the tide, or is the Everglades already lost?
Video.title: "The Everglades Python"

Video.summary: Kyle and Jeff get bit by catfish in Southern Oklahoma with YouTuber Nate Williams of Adrenaline Rush Adventures while using the most primitive fishing tactic - hand fishing. Learn from the Chickasaw Nation about the history of this method that has been used by Native Americans for thousands of years.
Video.title: "Oklahoma Catfish Noodling"

Video.summary: Michael Pitts of Realtree Road Trips joins Anthony Virga and Matt Garger hunting the hills of New York.
Video.title: Spring Thunder - "Pitts Hunts New York"

Video.summary: The 2024 season of Spring Thunder is officially underway. David Blanton kicks off the season in southern Florida by hunting the beautiful Williams Farms with Southern Outdoors Outfitters. The action is hot and it doesn’t take long to get on some angry longbeards.
Video.title: Spring Thunder - "Gobblers Looking for a Fight | South-Florida Strutters | Spring Thunder"

Image.alt: Spring Thunder - "Gobblers Looking for a Fight | South-Florida Strutters | Spring Thunder"
Spring Thunder - "Gobblers Looking for a Fight | South-Florida Strutters | Spring Thunder"
Spring Thunder - "Gobblers Looking for a Fight | South-Florida Strutters | Spring Thunder"

Video.summary: What starts as a normal trip turns wild as Capt. Jared and Capt. Garrett get swarmed by Cobia.
Video.title: "The Prodigal Son"

Video.summary: Randy and Cody are wrapping things up on the Raven before bow season arrives in Missouri. Planting I-70 the plot where Randy killed one of his biggest Missouri bucks ever last year. Then Randy and Cody attempt to setup a new farm they are calling “The River Farm” they attempt to plant a food plot, bush hog, spray the plot, setup a new Titan blind, and setup a Hooyman dual perimeter fence all in one day before the sun goes down.
Video.title: "I-70 and The River Farm"

Video.summary: Tyler Jordan heads back to his college stomping grounds at Ole Miss to hunt with Athletic Director Keith Carter and William Jones. After a tough few days they were finally able to find some birds that were willing to work!
Video.title: Spring Thunder - "Ole Miss Double"

Video.summary: Join us for an unforgettable Women Go Hunting experience at Legends Ranch in Michigan!
Video.title: "Women Go Hunting"

Video.summary: With over 20 years of service, retiring as a Senior Chief Machinery Technician, Steven served abord multiple Coast Guard cutters, led critical missions, protected our nation's leaders, and earned the Coast Guard Achievement Medal for his outstanding service. Beyond the uniform, Steven has has spent decades giving back - coaching youth sports, mentoring young athletes, and living the values of service, leadership, and integrity. Steven's dedication didn't end with retirement - and neither does our gratitude. We were honored to have Steven at Thumbtack Ranch and are even more grateful for his service.
Video.title: "Bucks For The Brave 2025"

Video.summary: We are blessed today to hunt with Cole Dutton for the Inaugural Pierce Dutton Memorial Hunt. We are honored to spend a weekend with Cole and his family at the G2 Ranch. Disabled Outdoorsman has played a huge part in getting those less fortunate outdoors. We are truly blessed to be able to follow along and capture these great moments.
Video.title: "Cole Dutton"

Video.summary: Follow TTHA President/CEO Christina Pittman and her husband Matt this week as they tag along side Dr. James Kroll for his annual deer day! This is a great opportunity to learn from one of the best. We are blessed to be able to share his knowledge with other like minded outdoor enthusiasts.
Video.title: "Deer Day"

Video.summary: In 2010, Amberly Snyder’s life changed forever when an accident left her paralyzed from the waist-down. Her comeback story not unlike something you’d see in a movie…in fact it inspired one. Disabled Outdoorsmen USA contacted TTHA and set up this hunt and we were fortunate enough to be able to capture just some of Amberly’s story and her harvest of a beautiful Axis buck at the G2 Ranch in the spring of 2022.
Video.title: "Walk, Ride, Rodeo…Hunt"

Video.summary: Northern California is known for two things: its vast bodies of water and the potential to tap into some pure California gold in the form of bass. Whether it’s competitive tournament bass fishing or just being out for pure, unadulterated fun, Austin Bonjour has a passion like no other and stops at nothing to fulfill his drive to rise in the ranks of competitive bass fishing. You may have seen his name atop the leaderboards of the Apex Tour, Wild West Bass, or WON Bass, but this short film gives you a deeper look into the glorious yet driven mind of one of the West Coasts fishiest bass heads.
Video.title: "Blue Collar Bass Head - Austin Bonjour"

Video.summary: In this episode, Slade ends the saga with a deer he has watched for a few years, Pope. On a previous episode you saw Slade put in all of the work with feeder and food plot strategy to make the stars align. Make sure you watch that episode as well to see the behind the scenes work it took to bring Pope to 25 yards.
Video.title: "Pope"

Video.summary: On this episode of Hunting Land Man, Slade, Ryan and Bearcub take on the midwest! After some tough hunting, the group is able to make a move to get in position on a BIG midwestern gobbler! Bearcub was able to harvest him after working the bird for almost 45 minutes! Slade and Ryan also do some Pre-Deer season work, and establish where the fall food-plots will be!
Video.title: "Missouri Turkey Tour!!"

Video.summary: It's the next Grand Slam stop. The Spring Thunder crew hunts eastern wild turkeys in Georgia. They get there just in the nick of time. When they arrive at producer James Thompsons' farm, a screaming bird is waiting for them.
Video.title: "7-Day Slam: Eastern Turkeys in the Swamp | "

Image.alt: "7-Day Slam: Eastern Turkeys in the Swamp | "
"7-Day Slam: Eastern Turkeys in the Swamp | "
"7-Day Slam: Eastern Turkeys in the Swamp | "
